title: Method and apparatus for heat-treating reactor pressure vessel
abstract:
PURPOSE: To recover toughness of a shell part and to extend the service life of a pressure vessel by working a heater and a cooler to execute heat treatment to the shell part receiving neutron irradiation in the reactor pressure vessel and eliminating cavity developed in the shell part.
CONSTITUTION: Charpy impact tests in the case of receiving no neutron irradiation and receiving the neutron irradiation with a metal material, are executed and testing temp. difference of the metal material at the time when these absorbed energies is equal is obtd. as approximate temp. difference from the composition of metal material forming the shell part of reactor pressure vessel 4 and the neutron quantity radiated on the metal material. When the shell part of reactor vessel 4 develops the cavity with the neutron irradiation to make this part brittle, a control unit 10 controls a heater electric source device 6 and coolant supplying device 9, and by working the heater 5 and the cooler 8, the shell part is heated and held to the uniform prescribed heat treatment temp. in peripheral direction until the approximate temp. difference becomes almost zero. By this method, the shell part is made to have toughness before receiving the neutron irradiation and the service life of reactor pressure vessel 4 is prolonged.

circumferentially surrounds the heating device and the reactor pressure vessel of a body part, the body part to detect the temperature of the temperature detection sensor, the body part is formed of a metal composition, the amount of neutron irradiation, heat treatment temperature and the temperature detecting sensor based on a detection value of the experimentally determined base, the value detected by the temperature detecting sensor is equal to the predetermined heat treatment temperature for a predetermined time said heating device has a control device to operate the device for heat treatment of a nuclear reactor pressure vessel.
